question.text: A put option on natural gas has strike Rs 500. If the underlying price turns negative to Rs -200, what is the intrinsic value of the put?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Put IV = max(Strike - Underlying, 0) = max(500 - (-200), 0) = max(700, 0) = Rs 700. Even with negative underlying prices, the put option formula works correctly. **The Trap:** Rs 500 ignores the negative underlying. Rs 200 is just the absolute negative price. Put IV = Strike - Underlying regardless of the sign of the underlying. **Cross-Exam Context:** Negative underlying prices are specific to commodity futures (WTI April 2020 precedent). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This scenario showed why put options are valuable insurance — even in extreme negative-price events, put buyers are protected by the strike price guarantee.
